input.prompt: Black and white hand-drawn pencil storyboard sheet, classic film director storyboard, rough graphite sketch on clean white paper, 6 cinematic panels in a 2-column by 3-row grid, each panel a rectangular framed shot with a small hand-drawn number badge (1,2,3,4,5,6) in the top-left corner, consistent loose sketch line style across all panels, soft hatching shading, no color, no text captions. Setting: industrial plastic parts manufacturing factory. Panel 1, close-up: hands placing a worn old industrial plastic part on an inspection table, camera push-in. Panel 2, medium shot: an engineer spreading his hands over the part next to an empty document folder, 'no drawings' gesture, shallow focus. Panel 3, detail shot: a pencil concept sketch on paper lying right next to the worn part, both in one frame. Panel 4, wide establishing shot: a large workshop coming alive, workers at CNC machines, deep perspective, sense of scale and confidence. Panel 5, wide tracking shot: camera flying past several production stages in one motion - milling cutter, sparks, a pallet. Panel 6, medium shot: a 3D scanner sweeping a glowing laser line across the same worn part, side angle.
